data.description:
## Finding

The verification pipeline intentionally tolerates and repairs provider/model output variations, but a structurally divergent response can currently normalize to an empty result that is treated like a valid review outcome.

## Evidence

- `src/rebar/llm/verify_models.py:143-161` shows tolerant Pydantic parsing can ignore unexpected keys and produce an empty model.
- `src/rebar/llm/verify_models.py:26-32` registers the live verification model without enabling strict-only parsing.
- `docs/adr/0006-llm-stage-seam-contracts.md:14-35` records the historical risk of malformed output yielding misleadingly green reviews.
- `docs/adr/0006-llm-stage-seam-contracts.md:94-154` intentionally adopts tolerant expand-contract parsing, telemetry, and staged tightening.

## Remediation

Preserve flexible parsing, normalization, bounded retries, and provider-independent self-healing. A recovered, semantically non-empty response remains valid. A semantically empty response is never a valid verification result; after recovery attempts it must become an explicit indeterminate or contract-failure outcome. Telemetry must distinguish clean parses, recovered parses, empty outcomes, and unrecoverable responses.

Cascade flag: behavior applies across providers and model versions; avoid schema rigidity that converts recoverable format variation into review fragility.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Clean preferred-schema responses continue to validate normally.
- [ ] Recoverable non-empty responses from supported provider/model variations are normalized and accepted.
- [ ] Semantically empty verification results can never produce a valid pass or valid empty review.
- [ ] Empty-after-recovery and unrecoverable responses produce a distinct explicit indeterminate or contract-failure outcome.
- [ ] Bounded retry/self-healing behavior remains provider-independent and deterministic at the orchestration boundary.
- [ ] Telemetry separately counts clean parses, recovered parses, semantically empty outcomes, and unrecoverable responses.
- [ ] Tests cover representative alternate wrappers/field shapes, successful recovery, empty normalization, retry exhaustion, and provider-version variation.
data.title: Reject empty verifier outcomes while preserving resilient parsing
